preserveUserBrands: assets/brands/* except assets/brands/example/ (example is updatable, user brands are not)

featureFlags._doc: Runtime feature flags read at Electron startup. Do NOT rely on these for security-critical defaults — a stale version.json on an offline client stays at the shipped values. Security-critical changes must land as code pushes with a version bump.

notes:
v1.16.0: Creative Angles — the 10-angle strategist taxonomy (Hidden Cost, Failed Solution, Social Proof Pivot, Mechanism, Enemy, Identity Shift, Urgency of Now, Comparison Flip, Objection First, Insider) is now first-class across every creative surface. Every `AdBrief` carries a `CreativeAngle` field; brand guides can declare `preferred_angles` / `forbidden_angles` (with a schema-enforced contradiction check — a brand can't both prefer and forbid the same angle); and `CheckBrief` runs a uniform pre-generation audit that blocks forbidden-for-brand angles (IssueError) and warns on angle/format mismatches (IssueWarn). The scoring rubric gains two angle-aware checks (`angle_declared` as a core gate, `angle_format_compatible` as polish), and `CreativeRubricSpec.WithAngleCriterion` appends a vision-judge `angle_execution` criterion (weight 2) containing the angle's canonical rubric hints so the judge has concrete signal. Wisdom telemetry tracks angles as a parallel dimension to hooks: `WisdomAdEntry.Angle` rides into the anonymized ingest, and the aggregator buckets angle performance (ctr / cpc / roas / win / cpa / n) per format across all users — omitted entirely when no ads declare an angle so legacy installs aren't diluted. Ad names encode the angle suffix (`/ angle:{key}`) for round-trip inference, with case-insensitive alias resolution (`How_It_Works` → `mechanism`). REGRESSION GUARD: every creative-gen path that constructs an `AdBrief` must call `CheckBrief` before generation — forbidden-angle veto is a compliance fail, not a warning. Full test coverage: `angle_test.go` (17 tests), `format_brief_check_test.go` (per-format minimal briefs + angle checks), `creative_rubric_test.go` (angle criterion append + non-mutation), `brand_guide_test.go` (+6 angle schema tests), `wisdom_test.go` (angle suffix + inference round-trip), `wisdom-api/worker.test.mjs` (+3 angle aggregation tests). `go test ./...` and `node --test worker.test.mjs` both green.

v1.15.0: PWA + Inline Artifacts + Refine Harness. Phone PWA shipped a self-install flow — `pwa.merlingotme.com` is now its own subdomain (Cloudflare Worker), the QR modal pre-warms so it opens with no broken-image flash, the install-banner prompts iOS/Android users with platform-specific copy, and the voice pill went through three rounds of polish (iOS-glass body → brand-gradient dot → single-row dot+title with subtle sublabel and collapsed status text). The mobile relay fallback now surfaces inline in the QR modal so a user on flaky LAN sees "connecting via relay…" instead of a silent loop. Inline artifact gallery: every image / video / voice generation pipeline now emits a sentinel-wrapped JSON bundle that the renderer turns into an enterprise-grade gallery card right in the chat — no more "can you render those here?" round-trips. The Go binary, the parser (`app/artifact-parser.js`), and the renderer's `merlinUrl` are all idempotent on already-encoded paths after a Gitar finding caught a `%20 → %2520` regression that would have broken any filename containing a space round-tripping through the gallery (full test coverage: round-trip, mixed-encoding, literal-`%`-in-filename). Archive cards now show the human-readable model name under each title ("Banana Pro Edit" instead of `banana-pro-edit`) plus a quick-delete button on image/video cards. Brand-switch IPC was hardened against an in-flight `startSession` race that could leak the previous brand's config into the new session's first message. MCP gained reliability primitives (idempotency tokens, concurrency caps, envelope versioning) plus Meta-specific intent tools (`metaFindCampaign`, `metaFindAdSet`) and a rate-limit stress gate in CI. Voice recovers from ONNX backend init failures and truncated-webm bursts instead of silently dropping the recording. Skills now pin `banana-pro-edit` / `seedance-2` so Claude reports what actually ran instead of inventing model names.

Refine harness (Go side) shipped a major reliability + quality push: skip-judge mode for fast iteration, multi-retry fal preflight with jittered backoff (cures the thundering-herd ParallelFetch failures from the corpus sweep), scored-only pass-rate so SkipJudge runs surface real errors instead of silently inflating success metrics, fake-press-logo guard, and shell-locked image prompts grounded in edit-mode artifacts. Format shells hardened against residual gaps in `ugc_static`, `before_after`, `carousel_frame`, and `problem_solution` — the structural gaps that survived the v1.14.0 sweep got fixed in `creative_format.go:PromptAddendum` (per the format-shell-addendum rule, not in briefs or rubrics). Phase 2 corpus expansion (12 → 20+ DTC formats per brand) for the regression-sweep harness. Server-driven model registry with latest-per-capability resolution — when a new banana / seedance / claude family ships, registry routes through it without a binary rebuild. Relay worker allowlists `transcribe-audio` / `transcription` envelopes so the desktop voice tools can offload Whisper work to the relay when the local CPU is saturated. Six new SEO blog posts in `landing/blog-posts.js` (`gomarble-alternative`, `smartly-alternative`, `revealbot-alternative`, `madgicx-alternative`, `hyperfx-alternative`, `martha-ai-alternative`) for competitor-intercept search traffic.

v1.14.0: Decisions + Spellbook + App Hardening. Every kill / scale decision Merlin makes now lands as a signed DecisionFact in `activity.jsonl` — an HMAC-chained audit record that says exactly what changed, why, and which run triggered it. The Spellbook panel reads those facts and paints a one-line outcome under every spell row (e.g. "killed meta-ad 12345 · CTR 0.4% · spent $18.40" instead of a bare timestamp), so you can see at a glance what your autonomous runs actually DID without opening the activity log. Decisions also get an MCP queue registration + aligned trigger vocabulary (`ad_underperforming`, `ad_scaling_candidate`, `budget_exceeded`, etc.) so skills route to the right decision path. Phone pairing is now re-pair-safe — v1.13.x could throw `multi_device_pairing_pending` when a user re-paired an already-linked phone; the `/pair/mint` flow rotates the device binding cleanly and the relay worker is deployed to merlin-relay with the new endpoint. Scheduled-task setup (merlin-daily / merlin-optimize / merlin-digest / merlin-memory) now requires explicit user consent via a prompt card before any cron fires — no more silent task creation on first-run. App-surface hardening: new SSRF guard on brand scraping (refuses private-network IPs, metadata endpoints, non-http schemes), TTS validation rejects malformed voice IDs before they hit the worker, redact / tool tests cover MCP input sanitization end-to-end, spellbook logic extracted to its own module (`app/spellbook.js` + `spell-outcomes.js`) with 400+ lines of coverage, service worker carries a version constant so cached PWA assets bust correctly on update. Backend test coverage jumped across the board: router/merge (3 → 30+ tests), SEO + landing-audit (0 → 60), comms + creative AI (0 → 54), email + SMS preflight + TCPA + auth records (0 → 58 tests), blog validation, Wisdom API idempotency, plus httptest runtime coverage for tiktok / googleads / reddit / etsy. Three blog draft titles tightened to sub-70-char SEO-safe lengths. Vendor-cards validator now refuses docs_urls pointing at rate-limited API hosts (would've been a silent monthly-cron regression) and CRLF-normalizes check-mode comparisons so Windows contributors don't trip false diffs.

v1.13.0: Vendor Capability Cards — every integration (HeyGen, fal, ElevenLabs, Meta, TikTok, Google Ads, Shopify, Klaviyo) now ships with a standardized routing card baked into the domain skill: Pick when / Skip when / Killer features / Constraints / Cost / Output / Docs / Last verified. The LLM previously had action-level context (here are the actions you can call) but thin capability-level context (why HeyGen Hyperframes is the right pick for a multi-scene SaaS demo, or why Spark Ads beat branded-post uploads on TikTok); cards close that gap without bloating cold-start context (they live inside SKILL bodies, load on-demand when Claude routes into a skill, leave description budget untouched). Source of truth is `tools/vendor-cards/vendor-capabilities.json` (dev-only, does NOT ship via extraResources); `gen-vendor-cards.js` deterministically injects fenced `<!-- VENDOR-CARDS:BEGIN/END -->` blocks into the owning domain SKILL.md. New CI validator (`test/validate-vendor-cards.js`) enforces schema (≥2 pick_when, ≥2 skip_when, ≥2 killer_features per card), cross-repo action coverage against every `case` in `autocmo-core/main.go`, hedge-word hygiene, and generator-sync (editing the JSON without regenerating fails CI). Passive monthly drift detection (`.github/workflows/vendor-docs-drift.yml` + `test/vendor-docs-drift.js`) runs on GitHub infrastructure — never on user machines — hashing each vendor's docs_url, opening a draft PR when any page changes so Ryan can review the vendor's actual docs before shipping updated cards. MVP ships 8 vendors spread across merlin-content (HeyGen / fal / ElevenLabs), merlin-ads (Meta / TikTok / Google Ads), and merlin-ecom (Shopify / Klaviyo); new cards land through the same JSON → regenerate → validator → `/update` pipeline.

v1.12.1: Fact-binding production fix — the v1.12.0 rollout flipped the feature flag on but the renderer's fact-binding helpers were calling `require('./facts/facts-cache')`, `./facts/verify-facts`, and `./facts/chart-renderer` at runtime. In production the Electron renderer runs under `contextIsolation: true, nodeIntegration: false, sandbox: false` — there is NO `require`, NO `Buffer`, NO `fs` in that world. Every one of those calls threw `ReferenceError: require is not defined`, caught silently by try/catch, and fact-binding became a no-op for every paying user on v1.12.0 despite the flag being on. The fix moves all three facts modules into `app/preload.js` (Node context, loaded once at window creation) and exposes an opaque-handle bridge through `window.merlinFactBinding` — `createCache` / `closeCache` / `watchFactsFile` / `stopWatcher` / `runAllPasses` / `mountCharts` / `createTailQuarantine` / `tailPush` / `tailFinalize`. The renderer holds integer handles; preload resolves them to the real FactCache / TailQuarantine instances and runs the HMAC / pass-1/2/3 / chart-mount logic in Node. Uint8Array→Buffer conversion moves from renderer (where Buffer is undefined) to preload. `chart-renderer.js` gains a string-based `mountChartsInHtml(html)` analogue because DOM elements can't cleanly cross contextBridge. REGRESSION GUARD (2026-04-18) comments in renderer.js and preload.js explain why `require` and `Buffer` are forbidden on the renderer side; new `renderer-bridge-runtime.test.js` (14 tests) source-scans for banned patterns AND drives the full bridge surface end-to-end. Full test suite 90/90.

v1.12.0: Fact-binding rollout — every number Merlin quotes is now cryptographically tied to the tool call that produced it. Each platform / analytics / revenue call emits HMAC-SHA256-signed fact envelopes (content-addressed over RFC8785-canonical JSON, keyed off a per-session HKDF-derived vault key), and the renderer runs a 3-pass interceptor on every assistant message: pass 1 substitutes `{{fact:<id>}}` tokens for verified `<span data-fact>` HTML, pass 2 binds `<div data-chart-config>` placeholders to signed chart payloads rendered as inline SVG (zero deps — no Chart.js weight in the installer), pass 3 scans any bare numeric literal not already bound to a fact and quarantines the suffix until the streaming tail either finalizes or trips the 2s slow-drip deadline. Send-boundary verifier re-checks HTML / plain / Slack blocks / Discord embeds against the signed fact log before anything leaves the app — fabricated ROAS, revenue, CAC, CTR, growth %, multipliers, and implied derivations all get flagged. `featureFlags.factBinding` flips on by default in this release (dual-switch override still available via window.__merlinFactBindingForceOn or MERLIN_FACT_BINDING=1 for kill-switch rollback). New protected-path patterns in block-api-bypass.js cover `.merlin-facts-key` and `.merlin-facts.<sessionID>.jsonl` (plus .bak/.tmp siblings per Rule 7) so the HMAC key + signed envelope log can't be read, copied, or rewritten mid-session. Chart renderer's empty-data fallback now escapes the title (XSS regression guard test added). Streaming boundary tests cover split tokens, split currency literals, tail-flush, and 2s slow-drip adversarial inputs; perf gate on the verifier hits P50 0.14ms / P95 0.35ms / P99 0.48ms on a 40-fact / 5000-word / 3-table / 1-chart fixture (budget was P50≤20 / P95≤100 / P99≤250). Goal: zero hallucinated metrics reach a paying user, full stop.

v1.11.1: Foreplay tile in the Magic panel. The competitor-ad-spying integration shipped in v1.8.0 was accessible only by hand-editing merlin-config.json — now it's a BYOK tile in the Universal section (one key covers every brand). Click to paste your Foreplay API key (link opens the key-management page in your browser), right-click a connected tile to disconnect. Matches the fal/elevenlabs/heygen/arcads pattern: `foreplayApiKey` added to the renderer's API_KEY_PLATFORMS map + main.js CONFIG_FIELD_ALLOWLIST + getConnections detection + disconnect-platform keyMap + GLOBAL_KEYS_SET so disconnects scope correctly to the global config. No binary changes required — foreplay.go already reads `cfg.ForeplayAPIKey`, so existing users who previously set the key manually see the tile turn green immediately on upgrade.

v1.11.0: Variant Tournament skill — adversarial creative iteration that beats LLM self-revision drift. New `merlin-tournament` SKILL routes a four-role loop (critic teardown → blind author who never sees the incumbent → synthesizer producing A / AB-merge / B → 3 blind judges scoring via Borda count with shuffled labels) plus a hard k=2 stop rule (incumbent wins twice → converged, stop iterating). The point: when a user wants to *beat* an existing winning ad, the old `write me 10 variations` flow produced cosmetic rewrites of the same hook because the LLM anchors to whatever it sees. Withholding the incumbent from Author B forces a genuinely different angle; anonymized labels keep judges from anchoring to the original; the k=2 stop rule prevents endless `improvement` rounds that flatten brand voice. Knowledge layer assembles from `dashboard` (current numbers), `dashboard wisdom` (cross-user benchmarks), brand `memory.md` + `brand.md` (customer language), and `competitor_spy` longest-running ads (proven-spend swipe file) — all four cached per tournament so every round sees the same baseline. Cross-references wired in `merlin-ads` Promotion Gate (route challenger generation here, not back to `merlin-content`) and `commands/merlin.md` fuzzy-intent table. Anti-drift guarantees are non-negotiable rules in the skill body — bypassing any of them defeats the pattern.

v1.10.0: Google Merchant Center integration — the missing piece for Google Shopping ads. One `google-login` now grants three scopes in a single consent (Ads + Search Console + Merchant Center via `auth/content`); the Merchant Center ID is auto-discovered and persisted as `googleMerchantId`. Four new MCP actions under `mcp__merlin__google_ads`: `merchant-status` (approved / disapproved / pending counts + top 5 disapproval reasons — the #1 reason Shopping ads silently stop serving, and the thing Google Ads insights refuses to surface), `merchant-setup` (MCA multi-client account picker), `merchant-sync-shopify` (maps active Shopify products → Merchant product inputs via the new unified `productInputs:insert` endpoint; skips drafts / items missing title, price, or primary image; maps `compareAtPrice` → `salePrice`; derives availability from `inventory_quantity`; idempotent re-runs via `offerId = shopify-{productID}`), and `merchant-insights` (per-product clicks/impressions/CTR from the Merchant reports API via MQL). New `google_merchant` rate-limit bucket (60/min, 1.5K/hr, 20K/day) keeps a catalog sync from starving Google Ads calls on the same account. 401 auto-refresh shares the `googleRefreshMu` mutex with Ads so a single token rotation covers every Google surface. Zero new OAuth providers, zero new consent screens — the expanded scope rides the existing Google login. Full test coverage: 11 merchant-specific tests including a regression guard that fails the build if the `auth/content` scope gets dropped from the OAuth factory.

v1.9.0: Auto-generated brand guides at onboarding — a hidden Electron scrape captures palette/typography/logo/copy samples, Claude synthesizes a schema-enforced brand-guide.json via the new merlin-brand-guide skill (anti-slop forbidden-word guard + WCAG contrast math + concrete-only palette/typography/voice/imagery rules), and the user sees a live review card (palette swatches, font specimens rendered in real Google Fonts, voice DO/DON'T cards, email preview using their exact CTA shape + colors). Two new MCP tools: brand_scrape (BrowserWindow-based scraper returning weighted palette, typography stack, logo candidates, JSON-LD schema, copy samples, screenshots — default-elides the large payloads for context hygiene) and brand_guide (validate / write / read actions — write hits the Go validator for WCAG + forbidden-word + schema enforcement before atomic persist to assets/brands/<brand>/brand-guide.json). ipcMain read-brand-guide handler + preload readBrandGuide bridge so the review HTML can load the persisted JSON without direct filesystem access. Zero new API keys, zero new dependencies — Electron's bundled Chromium IS the scraper.

v1.8.0: Competitor ad intelligence via Foreplay global discovery. New `competitor_spy` MCP tool pulls ads for any brand worldwide WITHOUT pre-subscribing to them in the Foreplay UI — the agent routes exclusively through /api/brand/getBrandsByDomain, getAdsByBrandId, getAdsByPageId, ad/duplicates, and ad/{id}. Fills the US-coverage gap that Meta Ads Library's EU-only preview restriction leaves open. Supports ad-media download (video/image → results/competitor-ads/<ad_id>.<ext>), ad-duplicate reverse lookup, cursor-based pagination, and credit-usage telemetry. BYOK — user supplies their own `foreplayApiKey` (0.01 credits per ad returned). Rate-limited via PreflightCheck (30/min, 500/hr, 5000/day). Bearer key banned from any direct outbound call via block-api-bypass.js — only the binary can touch public.api.foreplay.co. Static-source guards pin the no-Spyder contract (foreplay_test.go:TestForeplayOnlyGlobalDiscoveryEndpoints) — a future refactor that routes through /api/spyder/* fails the build.

v1.7.0: The big UX + resilience release. OAuth tokens now auto-refresh across every platform on a 4-hour Electron watchdog heartbeat plus a per-platform refresh loop in the Go core — no more reconnect every time a token expires. Every brand remembers its own conversation thread and paints prior history on reopen instead of a blank welcome. Morning briefing card summarizes ad performance, content, revenue, and a recommended next move at session start. Starter chips on both new and returning flows, Undo toast for ad kills, image-only Share, Ctrl/Cmd+D mic toggle, Ctrl/Cmd+S narrate-last, Lavender Pearl light mode with a titlebar sun/moon toggle, branded purple/green ad placeholders replacing the old gray broken-image stub, Stripe read-only revenue reporting, Reddit organic prospecting with a 7-layer compliance preflight, streaming TTS (first spoken word in ~400-700ms vs 2-3s), and a SaaS-grade rubric upgrade across every skill. Cold-start warmup is parallelized and pre-warms the SDK subprocess in the background so first-message latency drops from ~1-3s cold to immediate. Dashboard JSON parses are cached on the async hot path. Shopify now requests write_content and surfaces scope gaps as a reconnect prompt instead of a raw 403. Google Ads tiles stay green reliably across disconnect/reconnect cycles. Auto-claim referral on first launch. Under the hood: skill-routing migration from monolithic prose routers to 8 SKILLs loaded on demand (~53 KB off the cold-start context), monthly audit fail-loud on missing secrets, CI asar size ceiling, rate-limit preflight hardening via NextSlotNano, hook blocklist atomic-write siblings, framework-file protection so skill descriptions can't be hot-rewritten mid-session. Wisdom-api migrations now run once per isolate instead of per request.
whatsNew: Creative angles are now first-class: every ad brief carries one of 10 strategist-grade angles (Hidden Cost, Mechanism, Identity Shift, Comparison Flip, Objection First…), and brand guides can declare preferred vs forbidden angles so Merlin picks ones that fit your brand's voice.
Angle performance rides into the cross-user wisdom system alongside hooks — Merlin now learns which angles win by format and vertical, so recommendations get sharper every release.

Pre-generation brief check blocks forbidden-for-brand angles before the creative ever renders, and warns on angle/format mismatches (e.g. Mechanism angles on a Quote Card) so you don't waste a generation on a shape that won't land.
